//db: DB connection string is built at startup from POSTGRES_HOST / POSTGRES_PORT / POSTGRES_DB / POSTGRES_USER / POSTGRES_PASSWORD environment variables. Source these from .env (gitignored) before launching the API — start.sh handles this automatically.
//videos: Bootstrap-only — used to seed a Default VideoSet on first startup; runtime VideoSets in the DB are authoritative thereafter. Override per-machine via .env (VideoStorage__Root and VideoStorage__ThumbnailsDirectory). Empty here so the tracked default reveals nothing about your filesystem.
//encode: Encode/Convert profile (issue #164). Backend 'ffmpeg' uses FfmpegArgs (a raw argument string inserted between -i <input> and the .mp4 output). Backend 'handbrake' uses HandBrakeCLI with HandBrakePresetFile + HandBrakePresetName (requires HandBrakeCLI on PATH). Override per-machine via .env (Encode__Backend, Encode__FfmpegArgs, etc.).
//network: Access controls (#124). RestrictByIp=false by default (off). When true, only client IPs in AllowedCidrs (loopback is always allowed) can reach the app; everyone else gets 403 before any page loads. Add your LAN — a whole subnet (192.168.1.0/24) or specific device IPs (192.168.1.50). ForceHttps=false by default; when true the app redirects HTTP->HTTPS and (outside Development) emits HSTS — turn it on only once Kestrel has an HTTPS endpoint + certificate (see #222 / docs/lan-https-deployment.md). Override all via .env, e.g. Network__RestrictByIp=true, Network__AllowedCidrs__0=192.168.1.0/24, Network__ForceHttps=true. Behind a reverse proxy, enable ForwardedHeaders so the real client IP is used.
//auth: Keycloak JWT auth (#124). Disabled by default — set Auth:Enabled=true (e.g. Auth__Enabled=true in .env) once Keycloak is running. When on: /api requires a valid Bearer token, and writes require the 'admin' realm role (the 'viewer' role is read-only). Authority must equal the token issuer the browser used: for LAN access set Auth__Authority to your host's LAN URL (e.g. http://192.168.4.38:8080/realms/sightsandsounds) AND pin Keycloak's issuer to the same host (KC_HOSTNAME in docker-compose) — otherwise only the host itself (localhost) can log in. RequireHttpsMetadata stays false for local/LAN HTTP; true behind TLS.
